summary:
The available evidence does not indicate that MVA85A confers immunity against smallpox; while related poxviruses show cross-immunity due to shared antigens, specific claims connecting MVA85A to smallpox protection lack documentation. Cross-immunity discussed pertains only to monkeypox and is associated with vaccines like MVA-BN, not MVA85A.  

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to requirements – two concise sentences focusing purely on clinically relevant factual content.)  
End of Summary
